description: Designed whooo as 5-module library + thin CLI dispatcher rather than monolithic single-file. Rationale: each module has a single responsibility (parse / validate / walk / format / dispatch); easier to test in isolation; matches PAN's own pan-wizard-core/bin/lib/ pattern. Trade-off: 6 files vs 1.

Wrote DESIGN_SPEC.md before any code. Locked the YAML subset, error code contract, and module boundaries. Wave 1 implementation referenced this spec rather than inventing structure ad-hoc. Discovered later that one scope cut (block-style lists) was wrong — but the spec made the cut explicit so the deviation was visible.

Used handwritten frontmatter parser (zero deps) rather than pulling in js-yaml. Rationale: matches idea constraint 'zero runtime deps'. Risk acknowledged: real-world YAML edge cases will surface during dogfood. Risk materialized — see trace event at 11:50.

Tests assert violation SHAPE (codes + field names + severity), not exact string messages. This generalizes the timestamp-shape pattern (P-001 from prior whooo run) — assert the contract, not the prose.
Reused PAN's existing toPosix() pattern (utils.cjs) for cross-platform path handling in walk.js. Did not re-discover the pattern; recognized it from PAN's codebase.

globToRegex bug: '**/foo.md' regex required at least one '/' in the path, so 'foo.md' at root didn't match. Test 'globToRegex: ** matches across directory separators' caught it. Fix: when '**' is followed by '/', emit '(?:.*/)?' so the slash is optional. Mirrors gitignore/minimatch convention.

Dogfood gate produced 252 errors against PAN's commands/pan/. Cause: parser supports flow-style lists ([a, b]) but rejects block-style lists (- a, - b). Block-style is the dominant real-world format — DESIGN_SPEC scope-cut was wrong. Fix: extended parser to recognize block-list shape under top-level keys.

Per deviation rule R1 (bug discovered during exec), patched parser inline rather than deferring. Added regression test ensuring block-list parsing works against PAN-style fixture. Added inline comment in source pointing to this trace event for future readers.

Dogfood revealed schema's name pattern '^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$' is too strict — PAN files use 'pan:foo' format with colon. Schema authored from imagination; should have been inferred from real files. Fixed pattern to '^(pan:)?[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$'. Suggests a missing capability: 'whooo schema generate <dir>' to infer schemas from existing files.
Schema lacked 'argument-hint' field, present in 30+ PAN command files. 30+ unknown-field warnings. Schemas need to be exhaustive against the corpus they'll lint, otherwise they generate noise.

No prior promoted pattern for 'always test parsers against real-world fixtures, not invented ones'. This is a strong generalization candidate — caught real bugs in 5 minutes that would have shipped otherwise.
Phase complete. 39 tests pass; 9 real findings identified in PAN's actual files; all 10 REQs verified; dogfood gate produces actionable report. Status: passed.
